steps.title: Find the slope from the table
steps.content: For a linear function, the slope $m$ is the constant rate of change between any two table rows: $m = \frac{\Delta g(x)}{\Delta x}$. Compute it from any two rows in the table.

steps.title: Solve for $b$
steps.content:
Once $m$ is known, plug any $(x, g(x))$ pair into $g(x) = mx + b$ and solve: $b = g(x) - mx$. With the values from the table, this gives $b = 37$.

The answer is 37.
